title: Implement activity dispatch via conversation
purpose: Activity dispatch through liminal conversations instead of gRPC. This is the core integration: when a workflow calls schedule_activity, the integration layer opens a dispatch conversation on the task queue channel (ADR-007), selects a worker via the routing function, links to the selected worker process (ADR-001), and maps conversation lifecycle to Aion activity states. Worker crash is detected via process link, not heartbeat (sub-millisecond vs seconds). All channel operations are recorded by the Aion recorder for deterministic replay during workflow recovery. The zero-hop embedded path (ADR-010) ensures co-located dispatch incurs no serialization or network boundary.
task: Create dispatch.rs and codec.rs in crates/liminal/src/aion/. dispatch.rs implements the dispatch conversation lifecycle: open a conversation on the task queue channel, select a worker using the channel's routing function, link to the worker process, send the ActivityRequest, wait for the ActivityResult, and handle worker crash via process link exit signal. codec.rs defines the typed channel message types for dispatch (DispatchRequest, DispatchResponse, DispatchAck) with validation per ADR-002. The dispatch conversation maps its lifecycle states to Aion's activity state machine (opened->Scheduled, selected->Started, response->Completed, link failure->Failed). All channel operations must be recorder-compatible (constraint CN6). The zero-hop path must work for in-process dispatch (constraint CN5 still requires conversation mediation -- no direct function calls to workers). This brief does NOT implement retry policy (Aion owns that), worker registration (AION-005), or modifications to Aion engine internals.

requirements.title: Dispatch conversation lifecycle
requirements.spec: WHEN a workflow calls schedule_activity, THE SYSTEM SHALL open a dispatch conversation on the task queue channel named by dispatch_channel(namespace, task_queue). The conversation SHALL select a worker from the channel's subscriber pool via the routing function, link to the selected worker process, send the ActivityRequest, and wait for an ActivityResult. WHEN the worker responds with ActivityResult::Completed, THE SYSTEM SHALL close the conversation and return the result. WHEN the worker responds with ActivityResult::Failed, THE SYSTEM SHALL close the conversation and propagate the error. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT call worker functions directly -- all dispatch goes through a conversation (constraint CN5).
acceptance: Calling dispatch_activity(namespace, task_queue, request) opens a conversation on the channel aion.dispatch.{namespace}.{task_queue}
The conversation selects a worker via the routing function (not random, not round-robin hardcoded -- uses the channel's configured routing)
The conversation links to the selected worker process before sending the request
A successful activity returns Ok(ActivityResult::Completed { output }) to the caller
A failed activity returns Err(AionSurfaceError::DispatchFailed { .. }) with the worker's error context
No code path calls a worker function directly -- all paths go through conversation open/send/receive/close

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:1 — empty target module; implement `dispatch_activity` and supporting dispatch conversation types here, keeping file under 500 lines.
crates/liminal/src/aion/channels.rs:29-39 — `dispatch_channel(namespace, task_queue)` is the only allowed constructor for `aion.dispatch.{namespace}.{task_queue}` names.
crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:14-36 — existing `ActivityRequest`/`ActivityResult` vocabulary to send and return, including cloneable failed result shape.
crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:4-28 — existing `DispatchFailed` and `WorkerCrashed` variants to use when worker selection/conversation/link handling fails.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:43-63,355-378 — `ConversationSupervisor::spawn` creates a conversation actor and links configured participants through beamr links during boot.
context: `dispatch.rs` is currently blank; no direct worker call path exists yet, so introduce only conversation-mediated APIs and tests that would fail if a worker function hook is called directly.
Channel names are already newtyped and validated; use `dispatch_channel` and convert via `String::from(channel_name.clone())`/`as_str()`, not `format!("aion.dispatch...")` in dispatch code.

Conversation actors link only the participants supplied in `ConversationConfig`; worker selection must happen before spawning/opening the linked conversation or via a new local dispatch context that spawns with the selected `ParticipantPid`.

`AionSurfaceError::DispatchFailed` requires `workflow_id`; `ActivityRequest` has no workflow id today, so the public dispatch signature must either accept workflow/correlation context or set an explicit integration placeholder rather than losing context.
scout.approach: Implement `dispatch_activity(namespace, task_queue, request)` as the public façade in `aion/dispatch.rs`, backed by a small `DispatchContext`/trait dependency that supplies the channel handle, routing function, subscriber/worker pool snapshot, and `ConversationSupervisor`. The function should construct the dispatch channel with `dispatch_channel`, record/open a conversation, build a routing message from the request, call the configured routing function to choose a worker, spawn a `ConversationConfig` with that worker `ParticipantPid` and `CrashPolicy::RouteToNext`, send a typed `DispatchRequest` envelope, receive a typed `DispatchResponse`, close the conversation, and map `ActivityResult::Completed` to `Ok(ActivityResult::Completed { .. })` while mapping worker failure to `Err(AionSurfaceError::DispatchFailed { .. })`. Keep selection pluggable through existing routing abstractions rather than random/round-robin logic.
scout.notes: There is no channel registry or worker registration implementation in this branch, and AION-005 owns capacity/registration, so avoid building a real registry in this brief. Define minimal test doubles/interfaces in `dispatch.rs` so acceptance can prove conversation lifecycle and routing use without modifying `channel` internals.

files_changed.note: Added dispatch_activity façade, dispatch_activity_with_context, lifecycle loop, routing adapter, and conversation/recorder boundaries.
Added public dispatch worker, conversation, router, recorder, and operation types.
Added embedded no-worker defaults for the public dispatch façade.
Added dispatch test doubles for worker pool, router, conversation, recorder, and fixed ids.

dev.how: dispatch_activity constructs the dispatch channel exclusively via dispatch_channel(namespace, task_queue), opens a dispatch conversation through DispatchConversationFactory, records ActivityScheduled, obtains a current worker snapshot, selects a worker through the configured DispatchRouter, links before send, sends a typed DispatchRequest, receives a typed DispatchResponse, records completion/failure, closes the conversation, and maps ActivityResult::Failed into AionSurfaceError::DispatchFailed. No worker function hook exists in the implementation; the only worker interaction is through the conversation trait.
dev.deviation: The scout suggested spawning a concrete ConversationConfig with ConversationSupervisor where available. Because this branch has no worker registration/channel registry implementation and the scout explicitly allowed minimal test doubles/interfaces, I implemented the public dispatch lifecycle behind DispatchConversationFactory/DispatchConversation seams rather than constructing a real ConversationSupervisor adapter in this brief.

checklist.note: The dispatch path opens a conversation on the dispatch_channel result, selects through DispatchRouter/RoutingFunctionDispatchRouter, and calls link_worker before sending.

stories.note: Aion can dispatch an activity through the liminal dispatch conversation boundary with routing, linking, send, receive, and close lifecycle recorded.

requirements.title: Worker crash detection and re-dispatch
requirements.spec: WHILE a dispatch conversation is waiting for an ActivityResult, IF the linked worker process terminates (crash or abnormal exit), THE SYSTEM SHALL detect the termination via the process link exit signal, select another available worker via the routing function, and re-dispatch the ActivityRequest. WHEN no workers are available for re-dispatch, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Err(AionSurfaceError::DispatchFailed) with a NoWorkersAvailable cause. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use heartbeat timers, health-check polling, or gRPC keepalive for failure detection (constraint CN8).
acceptance: When a linked worker process crashes during activity execution, the dispatch conversation receives the exit signal and selects a new worker within the same conversation
The re-dispatched activity uses the same ActivityRequest (same input, same activity type)
When no workers remain in the subscriber pool after a crash, the dispatch returns Err(AionSurfaceError::DispatchFailed) with cause indicating no available workers
grep -rn 'heartbeat\|health.check\|keepalive' dispatch.rs returns no matches
Worker crash detection latency is bounded by process link notification (no timer delay)

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:1 — add crash/re-dispatch loop here; ensure no heartbeat/health-check/keepalive vocabulary appears.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:128-150,219-233,335-347 — `CrashPolicy::RouteToNext` and `ConversationContextEntry::ParticipantCrashed` already model retryable participant crashes.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:455-477 — participant exit is handled by recording the crash; only `CrashPolicy::Fail` drains pending receives with an error.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/beam.rs:124-153 — beamr EXIT tuples are detected and converted to `ParticipantPid` without timers.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/tests.rs:72-95 — test pattern showing crash detection via trapped EXIT message and immediate failed participant health.
context: Use beamr process links exclusively: conversation actor boot links to configured participant PIDs, and `beam.rs` handles `EXIT` messages from linked participants.
For re-dispatch, use `CrashPolicy::RouteToNext`; with `Fail`, `receive()` will be failed and the conversation phase becomes `Failed`, which is wrong for same-conversation re-selection.

The actor records participant crash in state but does not itself pick another participant; `dispatch.rs` must observe `ParticipantCrashed`/receive errors/state and run routing again using the same cloned `ActivityRequest`.

No-workers-after-crash should be surfaced as `AionSurfaceError::DispatchFailed { message: "NoWorkersAvailable ..." }` unless a dedicated cause enum is added locally; the current error taxonomy has no cause field.
scout.approach: Structure the wait path as a loop over attempts within one dispatch operation: clone the original `ActivityRequest` once, select/link a worker, send the request, then wait for either a response or a conversation state/receive result indicating linked participant exit. On crash, emit/record the retry-eligible activity failure state, remove or exclude the crashed worker from the candidate view for this dispatch, call the configured routing function again, and send the same `DispatchRequest` to the newly selected worker; if routing returns no selected worker, close/fail the conversation boundary and return `DispatchFailed` with a no-workers message. Tests should terminate a linked test process using `scheduler.terminate_process(..., ExitReason::Error)` as in existing conversation tests, not sleep/poll heartbeat state.
scout.notes: Existing conversation handles expose `query_state()` but not an event stream, and `receive()` blocks when no message exists. The implementer may need a dispatch-specific test double or a small non-core wrapper to make crash observation deterministic without adding timers; do not modify liminal core primitives per boundary.

files_changed.note: Added same-dispatch re-selection loop for DispatchConversationEvent::WorkerExited.
Added linked worker exit re-dispatch and no-workers-after-exit tests.

dev.how: While waiting for a response, the dispatch loop handles DispatchConversationEvent::WorkerExited as the process-link exit signal boundary, records ActivityFailed { retry_eligible: true }, excludes the crashed worker, re-invokes routing against the current worker snapshot, and sends the same cloned ActivityRequest to the next selected worker. If no eligible workers remain, dispatch returns DispatchFailed with a NoWorkersAvailable message. The dispatch module contains no heartbeat/health-check/keepalive logic or vocabulary.
dev.deviation: The scout referenced observing ConversationContextEntry::ParticipantCrashed from the core actor; this implementation uses the dispatch-specific DispatchConversationEvent seam so a future ConversationSupervisor adapter can translate beamr EXIT/link events without modifying core primitives.

checklist.note: Worker exit events trigger immediate re-dispatch through the routing function; tests assert same request reuse and no-workers failure.

stories.note: Crash handling is driven by linked-process exit events surfaced through the conversation, not a polling or timeout protocol.

Test linked_worker_exit_reselects_and_resends_same_request verifies two links/two router calls at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:91-122.

acceptance.criterion: Worker crash detection latency is bounded by process link notification (no timer delay)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/types.rs:206-213 models WorkerExited as the conversation event for a linked process exit, and crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:205-213 reacts immediately from receive() with no timer path. The grep above found no heartbeat/health-check/keepalive terms.

requirements.title: Activity state mapping
requirements.spec: The dispatch conversation SHALL map its lifecycle states to Aion activity states: conversation opened maps to ActivityScheduled, worker selected and linked maps to ActivityStarted, response received maps to ActivityCompleted or ActivityFailed, process link failure maps to ActivityFailed with retry eligibility. Each state transition SHALL be observable as a channel operation that the recorder can capture.
acceptance: Opening the dispatch conversation emits a state corresponding to ActivityScheduled
Selecting and linking to a worker emits a state corresponding to ActivityStarted
Receiving ActivityResult::Completed emits a state corresponding to ActivityCompleted
Receiving ActivityResult::Failed emits a state corresponding to ActivityFailed
Process link failure emits a state corresponding to ActivityFailed with a retry-eligible flag
Each state transition is a distinct channel operation (not a single batch)

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:1 — define dispatch state-operation records and emit one per lifecycle transition.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:170-183,271-348 — conversation phases/context entries to map to activity states.
crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:56-67 — `HistoryEvent` is the existing Aion event envelope shape, useful if dispatch state transitions are represented as event-like payloads.
docs/design/aion/design.json:6 — design text states lifecycle mapping: opened→Scheduled, worker selected→Started, response→Completed/Failed, link failure→Failed with retry.
crates/haematite/src/api/types.rs:1-10 — durable event payload metadata is opaque bytes plus sequence/timestamp; dispatch recorder events should include enough payload context for replay.
context: No `ActivityScheduled` enum exists in code; dispatch must introduce local observable operation/state names or use string event types consistently (`ActivityScheduled`, `ActivityStarted`, etc.).
Acceptance requires each transition as a distinct channel operation, not one aggregate record; do not batch scheduled/started/completed into a single event.
Conversation state already tracks sent, received, and participant crash entries, but it does not know Aion activity semantics; dispatch must explicitly map and emit activity-state operations.
Link failure should be represented as `ActivityFailed` with retry eligibility, but retry policy decisions remain out of scope; emit metadata, do not implement policy.
scout.approach: Add a small `DispatchOperation`/`ActivityDispatchState` model in `dispatch.rs` with variants or event names for scheduled, started, completed, failed, and retry-eligible link failure. Invoke the recorder/channel operation boundary immediately after opening the conversation, immediately after selecting/linking the worker, immediately after receiving `ActivityResult::Completed` or `Failed`, and immediately when participant crash is observed before the re-dispatch selection. Include `conversation_id`, `channel_name`, and `worker_id` where available so the resolver can reconstruct sequence deterministically.
scout.notes: Because `HistoryEvent` uses `SystemTime`, avoid generating new timestamps during replay; if you reuse it for recorded dispatch operations, make timestamp part of the recorded data and return it from replay rather than calling `SystemTime::now()` again.

files_changed.note: Mapped open/select/receive/exit lifecycle to distinct recorder operations.
Added assertions for scheduled, started, completed, failed, and retry-eligible failed state operations.

dev.how: DispatchOperation records one distinct operation per conversation open, worker selection/link, message send, message receive, worker exit, and close. ActivityDispatchState maps ConversationOpened to ActivityScheduled, WorkerSelected to ActivityStarted, completed response to ActivityCompleted, failed response to ActivityFailed { retry_eligible: false }, and worker exit to ActivityFailed { retry_eligible: true }, with conversation_id/channel_name/worker_id/result context included for deterministic replay.

checklist.note: Lifecycle-to-activity-state mapping is explicit and each transition is recorded as its own DispatchOperation.

stories.note: The recorder seam sees each state transition as a separate channel operation with replayable context.

Test asserts operation order at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:33-42.

requirements.title: Typed dispatch message codec
requirements.spec: codec.rs SHALL define typed message structures for the dispatch conversation: DispatchRequest (wraps ActivityRequest with conversation correlation), DispatchResponse (wraps ActivityResult with worker identity), and DispatchAck (delivery acknowledgement). Each message type SHALL implement the channel's type validation per ADR-002. codec.rs SHALL also define the content-type constants used for dispatch message serialization.
acceptance: DispatchRequest contains an ActivityRequest and a conversation_id field
DispatchResponse contains an ActivityResult and a worker_id field
DispatchAck contains a conversation_id and acknowledgement status
All three types implement Debug and Clone
All three types carry a content_type constant for typed channel validation
Publishing a DispatchRequest with mismatched content_type to a typed dispatch channel is rejected at publish time

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/codec.rs:1 — empty target module; define dispatch message structs/content-type constants/schema helpers here.
crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:5-36 — `Payload`, `ActivityRequest`, and `ActivityResult` fields that `DispatchRequest`/`DispatchResponse` wrap.
crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:47-109,328-401 — JSON Schema validation and tests for `Schema::new`, `validate`, and publish-time rejection.
crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:47-74,151-162 — publish path validates schema before delivery and rejects mismatches with `LiminalError::SchemaMismatch`.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-20 — `serde_json` exists, but `serde` derive is not declared; avoid adding derives that require a new dependency unless the brief allows dependency edits.
context: `codec.rs` is blank, so define content-type constants and typed message structs from scratch; all public structs must derive `Debug` and `Clone`.

The current channel validation is JSON Schema over bytes, not Rust trait-based typed channels; practical acceptance should be met by schema helpers that require a `content_type` property equal to the dispatch constant.

`ActivityRequest`/`ActivityResult` do not implement `serde::Serialize`; serialization helpers will need manual `serde_json::Value` construction or dependency changes if full byte encoding is required.
`DispatchAck` needs an acknowledgement status type; define a small cloneable/debug enum or stringly status, but keep it validation-friendly and public if used in schemas.
scout.approach: In `codec.rs`, define constants such as `DISPATCH_REQUEST_CONTENT_TYPE`, `DISPATCH_RESPONSE_CONTENT_TYPE`, and `DISPATCH_ACK_CONTENT_TYPE`, plus `DispatchRequest { conversation_id: String, request: ActivityRequest }`, `DispatchResponse { worker_id: String, result: ActivityResult }`, and `DispatchAck { conversation_id: String, status: DispatchAckStatus }`. Provide schema constructors (for example `dispatch_request_schema() -> Result<Schema, SchemaValidationError>`) whose JSON Schema requires `content_type` to equal the correct constant and the required correlation/worker fields; expose lightweight encode helpers only if needed by `dispatch.rs`, using manual JSON assembly around the opaque payload bytes/content types. Add a test that a `ChannelHandle` configured with the dispatch request schema rejects JSON bytes carrying the wrong `content_type`.
scout.notes: The brief says each type carries a content-type constant; Rust associated consts on inherent impls satisfy this without storing redundant fields inside every struct. If tests expect publish-time rejection, remember `ChannelHandle::publish` accepts bytes, so the mismatch test must publish encoded JSON containing the wrong content type, not a raw Rust struct.

files_changed.note: Defined typed dispatch request/response/ack messages, content-type constants, schema constructors, and JSON encode helpers.

dev.how: codec.rs now defines DispatchRequest { conversation_id, request }, DispatchResponse { worker_id, result }, DispatchAck { conversation_id, status }, DispatchAckStatus, content-type constants, inherent CONTENT_TYPE constants, manual JSON encoders, and JSON Schema constructors that require matching content_type values. Tests configure a typed ChannelHandle with dispatch_request_schema and assert mismatched content_type publishes are rejected with LiminalError::SchemaMismatch.
dev.deviation: No serde derives were added, following the scout note that serde derive was not declared; encoding uses manual serde_json::Value construction.

checklist.note: The codec wraps the existing ActivityRequest/ActivityResult vocabulary from types.rs without redefining it.
Dispatch message schemas enforce content_type constants at channel publish time.

stories.note: Mismatched typed dispatch payloads are rejected before worker execution.

acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/codec.rs:109-159 builds schemas that require the matching content_type; test channel_rejects_mismatched_dispatch_content_type verifies publish-time SchemaMismatch at crates/liminal/src/aion/codec.rs:298-311. Additional hardening tests at crates/liminal/src/aion/codec.rs:324-368 reject spoofed cross-message shapes.

issues: The original codec schemas enforced content_type and required fields but did not reject cross-message fields, so a spoofed payload with the right content_type could resemble the wrong dispatch message shape.
fixes: Hardened dispatch_request_schema, dispatch_response_schema, and dispatch_ack_schema to reject cross-message fields at crates/liminal/src/aion/codec.rs:109-159.

Added regression tests request_schema_rejects_response_shape_even_with_request_content_type and response_schema_rejects_request_shape_even_with_response_content_type at crates/liminal/src/aion/codec.rs:324-368.

requirements.title: Recorder compatibility for channel operations
requirements.spec: All channel operations in dispatch.rs (conversation open, worker selection, message send, message receive, conversation close) SHALL be structured as recorded operations. WHEN the Aion recorder is capturing, THE SYSTEM SHALL record each channel operation as an event in the workflow's event log. WHEN the resolver is replaying, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded channel responses without re-executing the actual dispatch. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT introduce a new replay mechanism -- it uses Aion's existing record-replay pattern.
acceptance: Each channel operation in dispatch.rs is wrapped in a recorder-compatible operation boundary
During replay, dispatch_activity returns the recorded result without opening an actual conversation
During replay, worker crash re-dispatch returns the recorded outcome without actual re-dispatch
The recorded events include enough state (conversation_id, worker_id, result) to reconstruct the dispatch outcome deterministically

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:1 — wrap open/select/send/receive/close and crash re-dispatch as recorder-compatible operation boundaries here.
docs/design/aion/design.json:6 — design explicitly says recorder captures dispatch conversations and resolver replays recorded channel responses; no new replay mechanism.
crates/haematite/src/api/event_store.rs:21-68 — existing append/read-from event-log style if a local event recorder test double needs durable semantics.
crates/haematite/src/api/types.rs:1-10 — event payload shape: opaque bytes plus sequence/timestamp.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:219-233,324-347 — conversation context entries already distinguish sent/received/crash events for deterministic record payloads.
context: There is no `recorder` or `resolver` module in this repository (`grep` found no code symbols), so this brief must define an integration seam rather than import an existing Aion recorder type.
Do not add a second replay engine; model a trait/boundary that Aion’s existing recorder can implement later, and provide an in-memory no-op/replay test implementation in this module.
Replay must short-circuit before opening a real conversation; otherwise recovery will re-execute nondeterministic routing/dispatch.
Recorded events need at least `conversation_id`, `worker_id` for selected/started/result events, and the final `ActivityResult`/failure cause.
scout.approach: Introduce a small recorder abstraction in `dispatch.rs` such as `DispatchRecorder` with `record(operation) -> ...` and `replay(operation_key) -> Option<RecordedDispatchOperation>` or a higher-level `DispatchRecordMode::{Recording, Replaying}` carried by `DispatchContext`. Route every nondeterministic operation—conversation open, routing selection, send, receive, crash, re-dispatch, close—through a helper that first checks replay data, otherwise executes the operation and records the outcome. Ensure `dispatch_activity` in replay mode can return the recorded completed/failed outcome without constructing `ConversationSupervisor`, invoking routing, or sending to workers.
scout.notes: Because the public brief signature does not mention recorder context, `dispatch_activity(namespace, task_queue, request)` may need to call a default context, with a second `dispatch_activity_with_context` used in tests and by Aion integration. Keep the simple signature while making real recorder injection possible.

files_changed.note: Added replay short-circuit and record_operation calls around all dispatch channel operations.
Added DispatchRecorder and RecordedDispatchOutcome integration seam.
Added replay test proving no conversation open/routing/worker pool call occurs during replay.

dev.how: DispatchRecorder exposes replay_outcome and record; dispatch_activity_with_context checks replay_outcome before opening any conversation and otherwise records conversation open, worker selection, message send, message receive/worker exit, and close. Recorded operations include conversation_id, channel_name, worker_id, result, state, and diagnostic message as applicable.

checklist.note: All nondeterministic dispatch operations are wrapped by the recorder seam and replay can return the recorded outcome without live dispatch.

stories.note: Replay short-circuits actual dispatch and returns the recorded activity result deterministically.

acceptance.criterion: Each channel operation in dispatch.rs is wrapped in a recorder-compatible operation boundary
acceptance.evidence: Replay boundary is at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:133-139; live operation records occur at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:147-155, 226-235, 248-256, 259-277, 280-299, and 327-341.

acceptance.criterion: During replay, dispatch_activity returns the recorded result without opening an actual conversation
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:133-139 returns replay_outcome before conversation_id allocation or open. Test replay_returns_recorded_outcome_without_live_conversation verifies no factory/router/pool/record calls at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:151-160 and 184-189.

acceptance.criterion: During replay, worker crash re-dispatch returns the recorded outcome without actual re-dispatch
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:133-139 short-circuits any RecordedDispatchOutcome, including errors. Hardened test replay_returns_recorded_failure_without_re_dispatch verifies a recorded NoWorkersAvailable failure returns with no live dispatch at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:163-189.

acceptance.criterion: The recorded events include enough state (conversation_id, worker_id, result) to reconstruct the dispatch outcome deterministically
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/types.rs:78-95 defines DispatchOperation with conversation_id, channel_name, worker_id, activity_state, result, and message; RecordedDispatchOutcome wraps the final result at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/types.rs:135-151.

fixes: Added replay_returns_recorded_failure_without_re_dispatch regression coverage at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:163-189 to lock the crash/re-dispatch replay behavior.

requirements.title: Zero-hop in-process dispatch path
requirements.spec: WHEN the Aion engine and liminal are embedded in the same beamr instance, THE SYSTEM SHALL dispatch activities through in-process channels with no serialization boundary and no network hop. The dispatch conversation still mediates lifecycle (constraint CN5) -- the zero-hop optimization eliminates network transport, not the conversation. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require configuration changes to switch between embedded and distributed dispatch -- the same code path works in both modes via beamr's channel abstraction.
acceptance: dispatch_activity called in an embedded beamr instance completes without any network I/O (no TCP, no loopback)
The dispatch conversation is still created and its lifecycle is still tracked (conversation open/select/link/close all occur)
The same dispatch_activity function signature works for both embedded and distributed modes
No configuration flag distinguishes embedded from distributed dispatch -- beamr's channel layer handles routing

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:1 — implement one dispatch path that depends on channel/conversation abstractions, not transport config.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:43-77,138-156 — `ChannelHandle` is an in-memory actor-backed handle; publishing/subscribing here has no network I/O.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:152-192,268-287 — conversation actor runs on a local beamr scheduler and sends commands via scheduler queue, not TCP.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-17 — liminal is already exposed as an embedded Rust library surface, aligning with ADR-010.
crates/liminal-server/src/server/runtime.rs:1-120 — server runtime is separate deployment code; dispatch should not depend on this standalone/network layer.
context: The current core `ChannelHandle` and `ConversationSupervisor` are in-process Rust/beamr abstractions; use them directly for embedded dispatch to avoid serialization/network boundaries.
Do not add an `embedded` boolean, `distributed` flag, endpoint, host, TCP, or loopback configuration to dispatch; distributed mode should be below the channel/beamr abstraction later.
The dispatch conversation must still exist even for zero-hop; zero-hop removes transport, not lifecycle mediation.

Codec/schema validation may still encode bytes for channel publish; the zero-hop requirement specifically says no serialization boundary for co-located Aion/liminal, so keep typed Rust structs inside the dispatch conversation where possible and only encode at typed channel publish boundaries needed by tests.
scout.approach: Design `dispatch_activity` to accept or construct abstract handles (`ChannelHandle`/routing/conversation supervisor) and never talk to `liminal-server` or any socket API. The embedded default context should use in-memory channel/conversation handles and route directly to local worker PIDs through beamr links, while the same API can later be backed by distributed beamr channel handles. Add tests with a fake transport counter or compile-time grep showing no TCP/loopback/server runtime references in `dispatch.rs`, while still asserting conversation open/select/link/close operations were recorded.
scout.notes: The current channel publish path stores payload bytes in an `Envelope`; if strict no-serialization is interpreted literally, avoid making dispatch rely solely on `ChannelHandle::publish(Vec<u8>)` for the worker request. A typed `DispatchRequest` carried through `ConversationHandle`/test doubles may better satisfy zero-hop while `codec.rs` handles external validation.

files_changed.note: Kept dispatch on abstract in-process channel/conversation traits with no transport configuration.
Added embedded no-worker default context with in-process no-op dependencies.

dev.how: The dispatch path carries typed Rust DispatchRequest/DispatchResponse values through DispatchConversation instead of relying on a network or serialized transport path, and exposes the same dispatch_activity signature for embedded and future distributed contexts. There is no embedded/distributed flag, endpoint, TCP, loopback, or liminal-server dependency in dispatch.rs; the conversation lifecycle is still opened, selected/linked, sent, received, and closed.

checklist.note: The implemented dispatch path is transport-agnostic and zero-hop friendly for embedded use while retaining conversation mediation.

stories.note: The same public dispatch function is used for embedded and future distributed contexts through injected liminal abstractions.

acceptance.criterion: dispatch_activity called in an embedded beamr instance completes without any network I/O (no TCP, no loopback)
acceptance.evidence: The dispatch path moves typed Rust DispatchRequest/DispatchResponse values through DispatchConversation (crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:238-247 and crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/types.rs:215-243), and embedded defaults are in-process seams at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/defaults.rs:49-94. Executed network/gRPC grep over dispatch.rs and dispatch/; no TCP, loopback, gRPC, socket, or endpoint terms were found.

acceptance.criterion: The dispatch conversation is still created and its lifecycle is still tracked (conversation open/select/link/close all occur)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:141-171 opens and closes the conversation; crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:187-203 selects, links, and sends. Test successful_dispatch_records_conversation_lifecycle verifies open/link/send/close tracking at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:14-50.

acceptance.criterion: The same dispatch_activity function signature works for both embedded and distributed modes
acceptance.evidence: Public signatures at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:103-122 take namespace, task_queue, request, and optional DispatchContext; there is no separate embedded/distributed API.

acceptance.criterion: No configuration flag distinguishes embedded from distributed dispatch -- beamr's channel layer handles routing
acceptance.evidence: DispatchContext fields at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:28-37 contain worker_pool/router/conversations/recorder/id dependencies, not an embedded/distributed mode flag; routing remains abstracted by DispatchRouter at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/types.rs:189-204.

requirements.title: No consumer group rebalancing in dispatch
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement global consumer group rebalancing in the dispatch path. WHEN a worker subscribes or unsubscribes from a task queue channel, THE SYSTEM SHALL adapt routing decisions immediately via the routing function without pausing or reassigning other workers. Adding a worker is subscription; removing a worker is unsubscription or crash -- no coordination protocol.
acceptance: Adding a worker to a task queue channel does not trigger any pause or rebalance of existing dispatch conversations
Removing a worker from a task queue channel does not trigger any pause or rebalance of other workers' in-flight activities
grep -rn 'rebalanc' dispatch.rs returns no matches
The routing function is called per-dispatch, not per-membership-change

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:1 — ensure routing is invoked per dispatch/re-dispatch and contains no rebalancing code or terminology.
crates/liminal/src/routing/function/execute.rs:90-122,192-229 — `SupervisedExecutor::execute` returns per-invocation `RoutingDecision` from current consumer state.
crates/liminal/src/routing/function/loader.rs:175-209 — `RoutingSlot::current()` gets the active routing function at dispatch time, supporting hot deployment without pausing workers.
crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:67-141 — subscription register/remove and resolve are immediate snapshot operations; no pause/reassignment protocol exists.
crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:34-45,95-110 — channel subscription add/drop affects active subscriber list without rebalancing existing subscribers.
context: Existing routing structures are snapshot/per-call oriented: register/remove update tables, `resolve` returns matching subscriptions, and routing function execution selects from current consumer state.
No global consumer group implementation exists; do not introduce one to solve worker selection.
Acceptance requires routing function per dispatch, not on membership changes. Tests should add/remove a worker then assert only the next dispatch calls routing with the changed candidate set.
Avoid `rebalanc` strings entirely in `dispatch.rs`; even comments/tests in that file would fail the specified grep.
scout.approach: Keep `dispatch.rs` membership-blind: it should ask the channel/routing context for a current subscriber/worker snapshot when a dispatch or re-dispatch needs a worker, invoke `RoutingSlot::current()`/`SupervisedExecutor::execute` (or a testable trait abstraction) for that one request, and then link only the selected worker into that conversation. Worker add/remove/crash should update subscription state elsewhere or be reflected in the next snapshot; do not pause existing conversations, move in-flight activities, coordinate partitions, or cache assignments across dispatches.
scout.notes: AION-005 owns worker registration/capacity, so model worker snapshots as inputs to dispatch tests rather than implementing subscription metadata storage now. Also avoid “round robin until later” shortcuts: the requirement explicitly rejects hardcoded random/round-robin selection in favor of the configured routing function.

files_changed.note: Kept dispatch membership-blind and invoked routing per dispatch/re-dispatch only.
Added test proving worker snapshot changes affect the next dispatch through a fresh routing call.

dev.how: Dispatch asks DispatchWorkerPool for the current subscriber snapshot only when dispatching or re-dispatching and then invokes DispatchRouter for that request. It does not cache assignments, pause in-flight conversations, move work between workers, or coordinate membership changes. The brief grep for 'rebalanc' in dispatch.rs returns no matches.

checklist.note: Routing is per-dispatch/per-re-dispatch and no global membership coordination code or terminology was added.

stories.note: Worker completion/failure is handled through the dispatch conversation path, while worker add/remove is reflected in later routing snapshots without coordination.

acceptance.criterion: Adding a worker to a task queue channel does not trigger any pause or rebalance of existing dispatch conversations
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:187-188 selects only when a dispatch attempt runs; test routing_is_called_per_dispatch_not_membership_change mutates the worker pool between dispatches and observes only the next dispatch routing call at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:225-250.

acceptance.criterion: Removing a worker from a task queue channel does not trigger any pause or rebalance of other workers' in-flight activities
acceptance.evidence: Worker removal/crash handling is local to the current conversation: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:205-213 handles WorkerExited by excluding that worker and looping in the same conversation, with no global membership or pause path.

acceptance.criterion: grep -rn 'rebalanc' dispatch.rs returns no matches
acceptance.evidence: Executed `grep -rn 'heartbeat\|health.check\|keepalive\|rebalanc' crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs || true`; stdout had no matches.

acceptance.criterion: The routing function is called per-dispatch, not per-membership-change
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:302-317 calls context.router.select_worker from dispatch_attempts, and test routing_is_called_per_dispatch_not_membership_change asserts two router calls for two dispatches despite a membership change at crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/tests.rs:225-250.

fixes: Moved routing adapter code to crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch/router.rs:10-86 while preserving per-dispatch routing semantics.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement retry policy -- retry decisions are Aion's responsibility via its retry configuration (principle P6).
SHALL NOT implement worker registration or capacity tracking -- that is AION-005.
SHALL NOT modify Aion engine internals -- the integration surface consumes Aion's public API (principle P1).
SHALL NOT modify liminal core channel or conversation primitives.
SHALL NOT implement a heartbeat or health-check protocol for worker liveness (constraint CN8).
SHALL NOT implement activity timeout enforcement -- Aion's existing timeout mechanism is unchanged.
verification: cargo test for dispatch and codec modules passes (unit tests for lifecycle mapping, crash re-dispatch, codec validation).
No file in crates/liminal/src/aion/ exceeds 500 lines.
All channel operations in dispatch.rs are recorder-compatible (verified by replay test that returns recorded results without network I/O).
dispatch_activity compiles and is callable with identical signatures in embedded and distributed configurations.
